summary:
A zero central line-associated bloodstream infection (CLABSI) rate is not sustainable due to intrinsic procedural risks and ongoing human errors that result in detectable infections globally, with reported rates consistently between 0.8 and 4.1 per 1000 central line days. Real-world data show only minimal reductions, never elimination, confirming that absolute zero is empirically unreachable amid operational complexities.  

\boxed{B}

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
